class Solution {
public:
    int reverse(int x) {
        if (x == INT_MIN) return 0; // because can't take absolute value, and reversed would overflow
        int reversed = 0;
        int sign = 1;
        if (x < 0) sign = -1;
        x = abs(x);
        while (x != 0) {
            int pop = x % 10;
            x = x / 10;
            // Check overflow before updating reversed
            if (reversed > INT_MAX / 10 || (reversed == INT_MAX / 10 && pop > 7)) {
                return 0;
            }
            reversed = reversed * 10 + pop;
        }
        return reversed * sign;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int reverse(int x) {
        if (x == INT_MIN) return 0;
        int reversed = 0;
        int sign = 1;
        if (x < 0) sign = -1;
        x = abs(x);
        while (x != 0) {
            int pop = x % 10;
            x = x / 10;
            if (reversed > INT_MAX / 10 || (reversed == INT_MAX / 10 && pop > 7)) {
                return 0;
            }
            reversed = reversed * 10 + pop;
        }
        return reversed * sign;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(x))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently reverses digits while checking for overflow during each step. It handles edge cases like INT_MIN directly and avoids 64-bit integers
